events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: personnel in swing radius of equipment (likely crane/excavator) during active sitework. OSHA 1926 Subpart O covers motor vehicles, mechanized equipment, and marine operations. Personnel in swing radius is a serious hazard. Need immediate controls: barricade swing radius, exclude personnel, stop work, etc.

Decision: fail. Risk: critical. Work stage: in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete: estimate maybe 50. Confidence high.

    "Personnel located within the swing radius of operating mechanized equipment, creating struck-by/crushing hazard",
    "No effective barricading or exclusion zone established around equipment swing radius",
    "Work proceeding without required control to keep workers clear of rotating superstructure and counterweight"

  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt equipment operations and clear all personnel from the swing radius",
    "Establish barricades or warning lines around the full swing radius of the equipment, including counterweight travel path",
    "Assign a dedicated spotter/ground guide to monitor the swing radius while equipment is in motion",
    "Verify operator visibility and use of audible/visual alarms before resuming work",
    "Brief all workers on the exclusion zone and enforce no-entry during operations"
  ],
  "rationale": "Personnel within the swing radius of operating mechanized equipment is an imminent struck-by/crushing hazard. OSHA 1926 Subpart O requires control of equipment swing radius to prevent workers from being struck by rotating superstructures or counterweights. Active work cannot continue until the swing radius is barricaded and personnel are excluded.",

    "OSHA 1926 Subpart O - Mechanized Equipment",
    "OSHA 1926.1412 - Operating near power lines and swing radius controls",
    "OSSHA 1926.600 - Equipment swing radius barricading requirements"
  ]
